schema.name: TZPID source truth generated from canonical equation master

identity.canonical_title: How to Choose κ for J₆ Dominance (Practical Guidance): J_6^2

theory.canonical_statement: - The Bessel \(J_n(\kappa)\) oscillates with κ; each order \(n\) has multiple local maxima. To make the 6th harmonic strongest, pick κ at a κ* where \(|J_6(\kappa^*)|\) is near a local maximum and larger than \(|J_m(\kappa^*)|\) for other m of interest. - In practice you sweep κ (modulation depth) experimentally or in simulation and plot \(J_n(\kappa)^2\) curves. The κ values where \(J_6^2\) peaks are your “magic” modulation indices.
theory.technical_interpretation: How to Choose κ for J₆ Dominance (Practical Guidance): J_6^2 is a symbolic expression, written J_6^2. It introduces a symbol or relation used elsewhere in the framework. It is built from J_6. Within the Trawin Zero Point registry it serves as one element of the framework's mathematical narrative.

formation.note: Introduced in the derivation where peaks are your “magic” modulation indices.
